statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 19 of 27 countries below the mandatory 90-day minimum in at least one fuel type, driven by Middle East supply disruptions and elevated oil prices.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure as oil stocks remain dangerously low across multiple countries. Romania presents the most severe situation, with all three fuel types in critical status - petrol at just 33.2 days, diesel at 32.4 days, and jet fuel at 76.4 days. Poland and Luxembourg also show critical levels across all fuel categories, while major economies Germany, France, and Italy are struggling with critical shortages in multiple fuel types.

The widespread nature of the crisis is evident in the numbers: 15 of 27 EU countries have critical petrol reserves below 90 days, 18 countries face critical diesel shortages, and 20 countries have critical jet fuel reserves. This represents a systemic challenge rather than isolated national issues. The EU average reserves of 98.9 days for petrol and 97.8 days for diesel provide little comfort given the uneven distribution and the 91.9-day average for jet fuel hovering dangerously close to minimum requirements.

Brent crude oil at $103.45 per barrel, up 1.51% and having risen approximately 27% since the current Middle East conflict began, continues to pressure both supply chains and consumer prices. Average EU petrol prices have reached €1.764 per liter, with some countries like the Netherlands paying over €2.27 per liter. The combination of high prices and strategic reserve releases indicates that Europe's energy security infrastructure remains under considerable strain from ongoing geopolitical tensions.
keyPoints: 19 of 27 EU countries are below the mandatory 90-day minimum in at least one fuel type, with Romania showing the most severe shortages across all categories
Major economies face critical shortages: Germany has critical levels in all three fuel types, while France and Italy struggle with petrol and jet fuel reserves
Jet fuel shows the most widespread vulnerability with 20 of 27 countries in critical status, averaging just 91.9 days EU-wide
Oil prices remain elevated at $103.45 per barrel, up 27% since Middle East tensions began, driving average EU petrol prices to €1.764 per liter
Only 8 countries maintain safe reserves across all fuel types, highlighting the uneven distribution of Europe's energy security burden
